Home.heroBody: A shelf for tiny frontend apps. It may check whether your computer is on, or turn your personal preferences into a heart-shaped poster.
Home.sampleBody: Put your birthday, drink, portrait, MBTI, and places to go into blocks, then make a very direct heart.

heartCardEditor.title: Heart Block Editor
heartCardEditor.summary: Put text and one image into little blocks, then arrange them into a very obvious heart.

powerOnDetector.title: Power-on Detector
powerOnDetector.summary: Press a button to seriously check whether this device is powered on.

qrFileBeam.title: QR File Beam
qrFileBeam.summary: Slice a small file into QR frames, then scan it back on another device.

minesweeper.title: Animal Crossing Minesweeper
minesweeper.summary: Classic minesweeper in pastel meadow colours, plus a devil endless mode that grows a new block when you reach a boundary.

HeartCard.metadataDescription: Edit text and an image to generate a heart-shaped block poster.
HeartCard.title: Heart Block Editor
HeartCard.description: Click any block on the canvas to edit it. The portrait block accepts an image, then you can export a PNG.
HeartCard.emptyImage: Upload an image on the right

PowerOn.title: {device} Power-on Detector
PowerOn.description: This tool uses browser, device, and graphics-rendering clues to seriously check whether your current device is powered on.
PowerOn.checking: Checking this {device}... checking... this may take a while.
PowerOn.result: Oh!!! Looks like your {device} is powered on.
PowerOn.metadataDescription: Seriously check whether your current device is powered on.

QrFileBeam.metadataDescription: Frontend-only QR file batch transfer and reconstruction tool.
QrFileBeam.title: QR File Beam
QrFileBeam.description: Pick a file and the browser slices it into a scannable QR stream. Large files split into batches automatically — finish one batch, switch the sender to the next, and the receiver reassembles, previews, and downloads.
QrFileBeam.batchSizeHelp: Frames per batch. Smaller batches scan more reliably; raise this for fewer batch switches.
QrFileBeam.qrError: Could not generate this QR frame.
QrFileBeam.senderHint: Each batch ends with file metadata + batch metadata + data chunks + batch settlement. The last batch appends the file settlement. After a batch is acknowledged, switch the sender to the next batch — the receiver clears its current-batch buffer automatically.
QrFileBeam.emptySender: Pick an image, text, audio, video, or other common file. The QR sequence appears here.
QrFileBeam.cameraError: Could not open the camera. Check browser permissions or use HTTPS / localhost.
QrFileBeam.scanError: This frame could not be parsed. Keep aiming at the QR code.
QrFileBeam.batchArchived: ✓ Batch {batch} / {total} archived. Switch the sender to the next batch.
QrFileBeam.lastBatchArchived: ✓ Last batch archived. Waiting for the file settlement QR.
QrFileBeam.checksumFailed: Verification failed: file info, size, or SHA-256 checksum does not match.
QrFileBeam.completeWithFinal: File reconstruction complete. SHA-256 verification passed.
QrFileBeam.waitingMeta: Waiting for the file metadata QR.
QrFileBeam.noPreview: This file type cannot be previewed in the browser, but it can be downloaded.
QrFileBeam.receiverHint: Keep the QR code fully in view. When a batch is complete it's archived and its buffer is cleared — switch the sender to the next batch when prompted.
QrFileBeam.exportPdfHelp: PDF lays out the current batch or every batch as vector QR pages for printing or offline archival. The all-batches ZIP can grow large for big files — pick what you need.

Minesweeper.metadataDescription: Classic minesweeper in Animal Crossing meadow colours, plus an endless mode that grows a new block when you hit a boundary.
Minesweeper.title: Animal Crossing Minesweeper
Minesweeper.description: Reveal grassy tiles to flatten them into sand, with numbers hinting at the mines next door. Endless mode sprouts a fresh block of meadow each time your flood-fill reaches an edge.
Minesweeper.rulesHint: Left-click to reveal, right-click to flag (long-press on touch). Click a revealed number whose flags match it and the remaining neighbours auto-reveal.
Minesweeper.endlessHint: Endless: when a flood-fill reveal reaches the edge of a block (specifically, a 0-adjacent cell next to a neighbouring block), the next block sprouts. Drag to pan, wheel to zoom.
